acceptance_criteria.criterion: summary.md gains a '## Reintegration' section, delimited by <!-- reintegration:begin --> / <!-- reintegration:end --> markers, written via rws generate-reintegration (machine-generated; rws update-summary rejects 'reintegration' as a section name since the JSON is the source of truth).

Formal section schema: schemas/reintegration.schema.json defines the required fields — files_changed (list of {path_glob, count}), decisions (list of {title, resolution, rationale}), open_items (carried forward to main), test_results ({pass: bool, evidence_path}), commits (list of {sha, subject, conventional_type}). Schema is the stable contract /furrow:merge consumes.

Auto-populated at worktree-complete: frw launch-phase on completion invokes rws generate-reintegration, which reads state.json, git log since branch point, and the latest review record to emit the section. Validated against the schema before writing.
/furrow:merge (deliverable 4) reads the reintegration section as its primary input for the handoff surfaced to the spawning session — schema is the interface contract, not prose.
Template added: templates/reintegration.md.tmpl for the initial section skeleton (used when worktree completes without prior content).
Test: a synthetic worktree with known commits produces a reintegration section that validates against the schema and round-trips through rws update-summary without data loss.

seed_check.evidence: Seed exists and is not closed: furrow-b0f2 (status=reviewing)
